bins.name: State jurisdiction must be preserved
bins.desc: The VSCC argues FERC has never regulated load interconnection and that asserting such jurisdiction now would violate the Federal Power Act and face legal challenge. It opposes any rule that shifts load interconnection authority from states to FERC or RTOs, and proposes instead that FERC set minimum standards while states retain oversight.
quotes: the Commission has never asserted jurisdiction over interconnection of load, large or otherwise. Load interconnection has rather been the exclusive province under the Federal Power Act of state regulators, such as the VSCC.
asserting jurisdiction over large loads where it has not previously done so

a range of commenters expressed concern with the Commission's assertion of jurisdiction, which both deprives states of the opportunity to address large load interconnection issues (which more than half the states are already doing), while subjecting any Final Rule in this proceeding to legal challenge under the Federal Power Act.

The approach described by the VSCC in its initial comments would ameliorate these concerns by maintaining existing state jurisdiction over interconnection of load, while allowing the Commission to ensure that the states are exercising this jurisdiction subject to minimum baselines that expedite interconnection as much as possible.

merely changing the regulator of such interconnections from state agencies to the Commission and the queue operator from transmission owners to PJM will not accelerate these interconnections if the reason for such delays is structural (i.e. lack of available generation or delayed transmission upgrades).

By implementing a minimum set of standards designed to remove bottlenecks in the interconnection queue itself, interconnections can be expedited while not disturbing existing state-federal jurisdictional boundaries.

If the goal of this proceeding is to accelerate interconnection of large loads, the most efficient means to do so would be to empower states to act, subject to a minimum set of standards established by the Commission.

bins.name: Calibrated deposits deter speculation
bins.desc: The VSCC supports commercial readiness requirements, including credit and collateral obligations, but specifies they must balance deterring speculative projects against discouraging real investment.
quotes: Commercial readiness payments, such as credit and collateral obligations, should be designed to appropriately balance real, customer-specific risk without deterring investment.

bins.name: Hybrid loads get fast-track rights
bins.desc: The VSCC proposes that large loads demonstrating system impact mitigation through co-location or curtailment should have a voluntary right to enter a separate, expedited interconnection queue managed by the transmission owner.
quotes: FERC should establish the criteria for eligible large load interconnections to voluntarily enter a separate queue when they can demonstrate system impact mitigation through co-location, curtailment, or a combination of both.

bins.name: State authority protects retail customers
bins.desc: The VSCC argues state regulators must retain authority to balance economic development against protection of retail customers, resource adequacy, and reliability, and that FERC jurisdiction would undermine this protection.
quotes: a range of commenters expressed concern with the Commission's assertion of jurisdiction, which both deprives states of the opportunity to address large load interconnection issues (which more than half the states are already doing), while subjecting any Final Rule in this proceeding to legal challenge under the Federal Power Act.

bins.name: Reform queue process not regulator
bins.desc: The VSCC supports minimum federal standards to remove bottlenecks in load interconnection queues and reform of the generation interconnection process at PJM, but opposes shifting the queue operator role away from transmission owners. It argues structural causes of delay require targeted fixes, not a change in who runs the queue.
quotes: The approach described by the VSCC in its initial comments would ameliorate these concerns by maintaining existing state jurisdiction over interconnection of load, while allowing the Commission to ensure that the states are exercising this jurisdiction subject to minimum baselines that expedite interconnection as much as possible.

merely changing the regulator of such interconnections from state agencies to the Commission and the queue operator from transmission owners to PJM will not accelerate these interconnections if the reason for such delays is structural (i.e. lack of available generation or delayed transmission upgrades).

By implementing a minimum set of standards designed to remove bottlenecks in the interconnection queue itself, interconnections can be expedited while not disturbing existing state-federal jurisdictional boundaries.

the Commission should act within its existing jurisdiction to accelerate interconnection of generation needed to support load. As the Commission has noted in several PJM proceedings, the generation interconnection process desperately needs to be reformed.

The Commission should direct PJM to do more, including additional processes like the RRI. Removing the causes of generation interconnection backlogs would go far in accelerating load interconnections.

state large load tariffs should also require withdrawal or capacity reduction procedures to ensure that projects that inevitably need to exit the queue or adjust injection/withdrawal from the system can do so with minimal disruption to other projects and the orderly interconnection of loads.

bins.name: Co-location earns expedited pathway
bins.desc: The VSCC supports a voluntary fast-track queue for co-located loads, where hybrid facilities pairing load with generation or storage receive a streamlined interconnection study process in exchange for installing system protection facilities.
quotes: FERC should establish the criteria for eligible large load interconnections to voluntarily enter a separate queue when they can demonstrate system impact mitigation through co-location, curtailment, or a combination of both.

bins.name: Curtailment commitments unlock fast track
bins.desc: The VSCC proposes that loads willing to commit to reliable, predictable curtailment during critical operating conditions should be eligible for the same voluntary fast-track pathway as co-located loads.
quotes: FERC should establish the criteria for eligible large load interconnections to voluntarily enter a separate queue when they can demonstrate system impact mitigation through co-location, curtailment, or a combination of both.

bins.name: PJM queue reform is urgent
bins.desc: The VSCC points to PJM's failed Critical Issue Fast Path process as evidence that RTOs are not better positioned than states to resolve large load interconnection issues. It urges FERC to direct further PJM generation queue reform, including expansion of the Reliability Resource Initiative, as the most direct way to unblock load interconnections.
quotes: States are better equipped than the RTOs to address these matters efficiently. PJM recently conducted an 'expedited' process (the Critical Issue Fast Path process) to attempt to address large load addition issues, only to have its membership reject each of the dozen or so proposals.

the Commission should act within its existing jurisdiction to accelerate interconnection of generation needed to support load. As the Commission has noted in several PJM proceedings, the generation interconnection process desperately needs to be reformed.

The Commission should direct PJM to do more, including additional processes like the RRI. Removing the causes of generation interconnection backlogs would go far in accelerating load interconnections.

bins.name: Minimum standards, state implementation
bins.desc: The VSCC's central proposal is a cooperative federalism model: FERC establishes a minimum set of standards for large load interconnection and transmission owners implement those standards through state-approved tariffs. This approach preserves legal durability, leaves oversight with state commissions, and sets a consistent national floor.
quotes: The approach described by the VSCC in its initial comments would ameliorate these concerns by maintaining existing state jurisdiction over interconnection of load, while allowing the Commission to ensure that the states are exercising this jurisdiction subject to minimum baselines that expedite interconnection as much as possible.

By implementing a minimum set of standards designed to remove bottlenecks in the interconnection queue itself, interconnections can be expedited while not disturbing existing state-federal jurisdictional boundaries.

If the goal of this proceeding is to accelerate interconnection of large loads, the most efficient means to do so would be to empower states to act, subject to a minimum set of standards established by the Commission.

bins.name: Structural causes drive delays
bins.desc: The VSCC explains that interconnection delays stem from structural factors such as lack of available generation and slow transmission upgrade timelines, not from which regulator oversees the queue. Minimum standards addressing queue bottlenecks are more effective than a jurisdictional shift.
quotes: merely changing the regulator of such interconnections from state agencies to the Commission and the queue operator from transmission owners to PJM will not accelerate these interconnections if the reason for such delays is structural (i.e. lack of available generation or delayed transmission upgrades).

bins.name: RTOs less effective than states
bins.desc: The VSCC cites PJM's failed expedited process and Order No. 1920's slow implementation timeline as evidence that RTOs and federal processes are slower and less effective than states at resolving large load interconnection issues.
quotes: States are better equipped than the RTOs to address these matters efficiently. PJM recently conducted an 'expedited' process (the Critical Issue Fast Path process) to attempt to address large load addition issues, only to have its membership reject each of the dozen or so proposals.

bins.name: Generation queue reform unlocks load
bins.desc: The VSCC argues that FERC's most effective action within its existing jurisdiction is to require PJM and other RTOs to reform their generation interconnection queues, since generation backlogs are a primary driver of load interconnection delays.
quotes: the Commission should act within its existing jurisdiction to accelerate interconnection of generation needed to support load. As the Commission has noted in several PJM proceedings, the generation interconnection process desperately needs to be reformed.

The Commission should direct PJM to do more, including additional processes like the RRI. Removing the causes of generation interconnection backlogs would go far in accelerating load interconnections.

bins.name: Voluntary fast-track queue for mitigation
bins.desc: The VSCC proposes a voluntary fast-track load interconnection queue for projects that can demonstrate system impact mitigation through co-location, curtailment, or a combination. Participation is voluntary, managed by transmission owners, and paired with a complementary fast-track generator interconnection study.
quotes: FERC should establish the criteria for eligible large load interconnections to voluntarily enter a separate queue when they can demonstrate system impact mitigation through co-location, curtailment, or a combination of both.

bins.name: Withdrawal rules protect queue order
bins.desc: The VSCC calls for state tariffs to include clear procedures for project withdrawal and capacity reduction, with financial penalties and capacity reassignment to discourage speculation and protect the orderly processing of remaining queue positions.
quotes: state large load tariffs should also require withdrawal or capacity reduction procedures to ensure that projects that inevitably need to exit the queue or adjust injection/withdrawal from the system can do so with minimal disruption to other projects and the orderly interconnection of loads.

bins.name: Technical conference requested
bins.desc: The VSCC requests that FERC schedule a technical conference to gather input from all sectors, including state regulators, and to develop the minimum standards framework through a fuller record before issuing a NOPR.
quotes: the VSCC believes that the record in this proceeding would greatly benefit from a technical conference that solicits input from all sectors, including state regulators.
